Package it.polimi.ingsfw.ingsfwproject.Model

Class PlayerGround

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsfw.ingsfwproject.Model.PlayerGround
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class PlayerGround extends Object implements Serializable
The PlayerGround class represents the play area of a player in the game. It has a ContentCounter to keep track of the visible resources and objects on the play area, a Map to keep track of the played cards and a ArrayList to keep track of the available positions in the play area.
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    PlayerGround()
    Constructs a PlayerGround instance and initialize the ContentCounter, the Map for played cards, and the ArrayList for available positions, adding the Coordinate (0,0) to it.

  • Constructor Details

    • PlayerGround

      public PlayerGround()
      Constructs a PlayerGround instance and initialize the ContentCounter, the Map for played cards, and the ArrayList for available positions, adding the Coordinate (0,0) to it.

  • Method Details

    • playCard

      public int playCard(PlayableCard card, boolean upwards, Coordinate coord) throws NotEnoughResourcesException, PositionNotAvailableException
      This method is used to add a card in the play area with the specified orientation and in the specified position. It updates the ContentCounter, the available positions Map and calculates the points given by this played.
      Parameters:
      card - the PlayableCard to be played.
      upwards - a boolean indicating whether to play the card face up (true) or face down (false).
      coord - the Coordinate where the card will be placed.
      Returns:
      the number of points earned by playing the card (0 if the card doesn't give points).
      Throws:
      NotEnoughResourcesException - if there are not enough resources in the play area to play the card.
      PositionNotAvailableException - if the specified position is not available for placing a card.

    • checkIfCanBeAvailable

      public boolean checkIfCanBeAvailable(Coordinate coord)
      This method checks if a Coordinate can become an available position. It is useful in those cases where, iterating on the played face's corners, a position seems to be available because the face has a corner there, but in the same position another card has a "hidden corner" which cannot accommodate other cards.
      Parameters:
      coord - the coordinate to check.
      Returns:
      true if the Coordinate passed as a parameter can become an available position, false otherwise.

    • calculatePoints

      public int calculatePoints(Face face, int coveredCorners)
      This method calculates and returns the point given by a played face.
      Parameters:
      face - the face played.
      coveredCorners - the corners covered by the card in playerground.
      Returns:
      the points given by the face.

    • getAvailablePositions

      public ArrayList<Coordinate> getAvailablePositions()
      This method returns an ArrayList with the available positions.
      Returns:
      an ArrayList with the available positions.

    • getGrid

      public Map<Coordinate,Face> getGrid()
      This method returns a Map with the player's grid.
      Returns:
      a Map with the player's grid.

    • getContentCount

      public int getContentCount(Content content)
      This method returns the count of the objects or resources passed as a parameter.
      Parameters:
      content - the Content whose count you want to know.
      Returns:
      the count of the Content passed as a parameter.

    • setContentCount

      public void setContentCount(Content content, int newValue)
      Sets the count of the specified Content to the given value.
      Parameters:
      content - the Content whose count is to be set.
      newValue - the new count value to be assigned to the Content.
